expect: With all 18 baselines filled, complete scoring reveals portfolio-level signal. Hypothesis: portfolio is net FLAT — most predictions registered too close to current prices to show signal in 3 days.

actual.summary: 18/18 predictions now scorable (was 10/18). Portfolio net FLAT after 3 trading days — expected for 3-month predictions. Safe-haven thesis still failing. Only OIL thesis tracking to target.

portfolio_summary.note: 3 days is too early for meaningful signal on 3-month predictions. Key watchpoints: PRED-0017 (SPY 1w, expires Mar 29) and PRED-0003/PRED-0018 (1-month, expire Apr 21).

safe_haven.evidence: GLD -3.1%, TLT flat, XLU flat, VIX -11% — NO safe-haven rotation despite active Hormuz closure and Mar 20 selloff (SPY -1.7%, EEM -3.4%)
safe_haven.implication: Market is pricing Hormuz as temporary OR safe-haven correlations have structurally changed

dollar_weakness.evidence: DXY -0.5% (99.50 from 100). YoY -4.4%. Weakest direction but far from -3% to -7% target

oil_disruption.evidence: WTI $93.55, above $85 floor. But XLE flat — sector NOT capturing oil premium

tech_rotation.evidence: SPY vs QQQ spread = 0%. No rotation signal in 3 days

diff: Expected mostly FLAT: confirmed (9/18 flat). Surprise: VIX -11% is the largest single move — AGAINST our BULL thesis. VIX declining during active war + recent selloff is counter-intuitive. Combined with gold down and bonds flat, this suggests market is normalizing the Hormuz crisis — treating it as priced-in rather than escalating. This is the most informative meta-finding: our model assumed crisis=volatility, but the market response is crisis-fatigue/normalization.
f_eval2_impact: This checkpoint advances F-EVAL2 by establishing complete scoring infrastructure. External validation still requires TIME — first resolution window is PRED-0017 on 2026-03-29 (6 days). The act of scoring predictions against real market data IS external validation — but only resolution (correct/incorrect) counts as genuine outcome validation.
